generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance BuildingInformationModelSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Enriched BIM data sensor capturing device models, operational parameters, and semantic metadata from BIM environments"
    DTDFVocab:producedData BimEnrichedDataTransmitted
]

instance IoTSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor capturing real-time environmental metrics, occupancy status, and system performance logs from physical building infrastructure"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperationalDataTransmitted
]

instance ScadaSystemSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "SCADA system capturing operational logs and control system performance data from building management systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ScadaDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing energy consumption metrics for predictive analytics and optimization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralIntegritySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing structural performance data for integrity monitoring and predictive maintenance"
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance BimEnrichedDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Enriched BIM data including device models, operational parameters, and semantic metadata transmitted from BIM environments"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BuildingInformationModelSensor
]

instance OperationalDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Dynamic operational data including environmental metrics, occupancy status, and system performance logs"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IoTSensor, ScadaSystemSensor
]

instance ScadaDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Operational logs and control system performance data from SCADA systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ScadaSystemSensor
]

instance EnergyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time energy consumption metrics for predictive analytics"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

instance StructuralDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Structural performance data for integrity monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralIntegritySensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BuildingInformationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static geometric and semantic model from BIM environments serving as foundation for Digital Twin deployment"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo IntegrationFramework, DataManagementSystem
]

instance BuildingControlSystemModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model for Building Control Systems including sensors, actuators, and controllers semantically enriched in BIM"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine, DataManagementSystem
]

instance EnergyConsumptionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data-driven simulation model for energy consumption prediction and optimization"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance StructuralIntegrityModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data-driven simulation model for structural integrity assessment and predictive maintenance"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuously synchronized real-time data from physical building infrastructure"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalDataTransmitted, ScadaDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataManagementSystem, VisualizationTool
]

instance HistoricalOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical data archive for trend analysis and long-term performance assessment"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataManagementSystem, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance BimEnrichedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Semantically enriched BIM data including device models and operational parameters"
    DTDFVocab:fromData BimEnrichedDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo IntegrationFramework, VisualizationTool
]

instance EnergyConsumptionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed energy consumption metrics for optimization and predictive analytics"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnergyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance StructuralIntegrityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed structural performance data for integrity assessment"
    DTDFVocab:fromData StructuralDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance IntegrationFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Systematic integration framework enabling interoperability between heterogeneous data sources and domain-specific models"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, DataSynchronizationService
]

instance SimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data-driven simulation engine for predictive analytics, optimization, and scenario testing"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService, OptimizationService
]

instance DataManagementSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Centralized database system for storing and processing real-time and historical data with edge computing capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, HistoricalAnalysisService
]

instance VisualizationTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D/4D modeling platform for immersive visualization of physical twin and real-time dashboards"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService, CollaborationService
]

instance CollaborationPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cloud-based platform enabling multi-stakeholder collaboration with version control and change management"
    DTDFVocab:enables CollaborationService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MDE tool for automated extraction of structured representations from BIM models and generation of deployable DT artifacts"
    DTDFVocab:enables ArtifactGenerationService
]

instance CybersecurityFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cybersecurity measures protecting sensitive operational and design data in the DT ecosystem"
    DTDFVocab:enables SecurityService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service aggregating and processing runtime data for up-to-date building performance insights"
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingPerformanceInsight, AnomalyDetectionAlert
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service using data-driven models to forecast asset degradation, energy trends, and maintenance needs"
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceInsight, EnergyConsumptionForecast
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service applying simulation-based optimization to improve operational efficiency and energy use"
    DTDFVocab:provides EfficiencyRecommendation, CostOptimizationInsight
]

instance ControlAndAutomationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service integrating with building automation systems for closed-loop control and automated workflows"
    DTDFVocab:provides AutomatedControlAction, MaintenanceAlertAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing interactive 3D/4D visualizations for stakeholder alignment and decision support"
    DTDFVocab:provides SpatialTemporalInsight, ImmersiveVisualization
]

instance CollaborationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service facilitating multi-disciplinary collaboration through shared digital environments"
    DTDFVocab:provides StakeholderAlignmentInsight, CollaborationFeedback
]

instance DataSynchronizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service ensuring continuous bidirectional data exchange between physical building and digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:provides SynchronizationStatusInsight
]

instance ArtifactGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service automatically generating deployable DT artifacts from BIM models"
    DTDFVocab:provides DeploymentArtifactInsight
]

instance SecurityService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing cybersecurity measures for data protection"
    DTDFVocab:provides SecurityComplianceInsight
]

instance HistoricalAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing trend analysis and long-term performance assessment using historical data"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalTrendInsight
]

instance DocumentationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing version control and change documentation for collaborative environments"
    DTDFVocab:provides DocumentationInsight
]

instance DeploymentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing deployment of DT artifacts and system updates"
    DTDFVocab:provides DeploymentStatusInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance BuildingPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time operational state monitoring of building systems and components"
]

instance AnomalyDetectionAlert : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated alert for detected anomalies in building operations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecast of asset degradation and maintenance requirements based on sensor data trends"
]

instance EnergyConsumptionForecast : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Prediction of future energy consumption trends and optimization opportunities"
]

instance EfficiencyRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Recommendations for improving operational efficiency through system adjustments"
]

instance CostOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of cost-saving opportunities through optimized building operations"
]

instance AutomatedControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of building systems based on real-time conditions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MaintenanceAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of maintenance alerts based on predictive analytics"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SpatialTemporalInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Spatial-temporal analysis of building performance through immersive visualizations"
]

instance ImmersiveVisualization : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D visualization of building performance data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance StakeholderAlignmentInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Shared understanding of building performance across multidisciplinary stakeholders"
]

instance CollaborationFeedback : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Feedback mechanism for collaborative decision-making processes"
]

instance SynchronizationStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Status monitoring of bidirectional data synchronization between physical and digital twins"
]

instance DeploymentArtifactInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Verification of automatically generated deployable DT artifacts"
]

instance SecurityComplianceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Monitoring of cybersecurity compliance and data protection measures"
]

instance HistoricalTrendInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of historical data trends for long-term performance assessment"
]

instance DocumentationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Version control and change documentation for collaborative environments"
]

instance DeploymentStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Status monitoring of DT artifact deployment and system updates"
]

// C2: Acting Components (from twinning process)
instance BuildingControlActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator component for Building Control Systems enabling physical adjustments based on digital twin insights"
]

instance EnergyManagementActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator for energy system control enabling automated optimization based on predictive analytics"
]

instance StructuralMonitoringActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator for structural integrity monitoring enabling automated alerts for maintenance needs"
]

// C1: Environment
instance AeCoDigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital Twin environment for Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations domain"
    base:contains BuildingInformationModel,
                   BuildingControlSystemModel,
                   EnergyConsumptionModel,
                   StructuralIntegrityModel,
                   IntegrationFramework,
                   SimulationEngine,
                   DataManagementSystem,
                   VisualizationTool,
                   CollaborationPlatform,
                   ModelDrivenEngineeringTool,
                   CybersecurityFramework,
                   BuildingControlActuator,
                   EnergyManagementActuator,
                   StructuralMonitoringActuator
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this DT system is governed by a **control-centric architecture** where the digital twin issues commands to the physical building infrastructure. Key aspects include:
  - **Command Types**: Likely include **real-time control signals** (e.g., HVAC adjustments, lighting regulation, security system activations) derived from operational analytics or user inputs (e.g., Facility Managers).
  - **Control Message Formats**: Standardized formats are implied by the framework’s **interoperability requirements (NF_X)**, but specific formats (e.g., JSON, XML, binary) are not specified.
  - **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**: Not explicitly detailed, though the framework’s **scalability and maintainability** requirements (NF_X) suggest robust mechanisms to prevent unauthorized or erroneous commands.
  - **Feedback Systems**: Bidirectional data exchange implies **closed-loop control**, where physical system responses (e.g., sensor confirmations, status updates) are fed back to the DT for validation or further adjustments.
  - **Conflict Resolution**: Likely handled via the **runtime configuration management** system, enabling dynamic adjustments without redeployment. Specific algorithms (e.g., priority-based arbitration) are not described.
  - **Command Execution Confirmation**: Implicit in the **real-time monitoring and control** workflow, where Facility Managers interact with the deployed DT to verify outcomes, though no explicit acknowledgment protocols are mentioned.
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, implying sub-second to millisecond-level latency requirements for critical operational data (e.g., sensor readings, control signals). The framework supports **dynamic temporal granularity** tailored to service-specific needs, such as high-frequency updates for monitoring (e.g., environmental sensors) and lower-frequency adjustments for configuration management. Runtime configuration management enables **on-the-fly adjustments** without redeployment, suggesting adaptive time scales for non-critical services (e.g., analytics or user interfaces). The system prioritizes **bidirectional, low-latency synchronization** to bridge static BIM representations with dynamic operational demands in the AECO domain, aligning with the need for seamless integration across the building lifecycle (e.g., from design to real-time control)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **coordinated, service-oriented constellation** centered on the DT Framework, which orchestrates interactions between **enriched BIM models**, **physical building components**, and **external services**. The architecture follows a **modular, role-based pattern** with explicit component integration:
  - **Bidirectional Data Flow**: The framework facilitates real-time synchronization between physical and digital layers, ensuring **holistic lifecycle management** (e.g., from design to operational phases).
  - **Runtime Configuration Management**: Enables dynamic adjustments to twin capabilities (e.g., adding/removing services) without full redeployment, leveraging **low-overhead updates** for maintainability.
  - **Resource Management**: The system prioritizes **scalability** from individual components (e.g., sensor data streams) to entire infrastructure systems, with **interoperability** ensured via standardized extension points.
  - **System-Wide Coordination**: The DT Framework acts as a **central hub**, resolving conflicts and managing dependencies between **Building Control Engineers**, **Facility Managers**, and **DT Engineers** through predefined roles and data exchange protocols. The constellation emphasizes **adaptability** to evolving operational requirements while maintaining **low-latency, high-fidelity synchronization** across the twin lifecycle."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define sensors, actuators, and controllers within the BIM environment, creating a **semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations. This phase leverages BIM’s static capabilities to model Building Control System (BCS) components.
  2. **Model Transformation**: The framework employs **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles** to extract structured representations from enriched BIM models, enabling automated generation of deployable DT artifacts with **minimal manual intervention**.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed, establishing a **real-time synchronized** connection between the physical building and its digital twin. This phase ensures continuous bidirectional data exchange.
  4. **Operation & Monitoring**: Facility Managers interact with the DT to monitor system states and operate physical infrastructure, utilizing **interactive visualization** for immersive monitoring and control.
  5. **Custom Service Integration**: DT Engineers extend functionality through **custom services and plugins**, integrated via the framework’s **modular architecture** with standardized extension points. This phase supports evolutionary DT capabilities aligned with changing operational needs.
  Transition management between phases is governed by the framework’s **modular architecture**, ensuring seamless interoperability and lifecycle governance through standardized interfaces and automated workflows. The framework’s design is validated through research projects like **TwinLight and BOREALIS**, addressing practical deployment challenges."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework addresses **fidelity and validity** through a **multi-layered validation and verification (V&V) process** tied to its **model-driven deployment lifecycle**. **Model fidelity** is ensured by enriching static BIM representations with **dynamic BCS components** (sensors, actuators, controllers), enabling accurate behavioral and analytical modeling. **Validation methods** include automated **semantic consistency checks** during MDE-based model transformations and **runtime synchronization validation** to confirm real-time data alignment between physical and digital systems. **Uncertainty sources** are mitigated via structured data extraction from BIM, though explicit quantification methods (e.g., probabilistic models or error bounds) are not detailed. **Error detection** is implicit in the framework’s **real-time data exchange** and **interactive visualization**, where discrepancies trigger alerts for manual review or corrective actions. **Quality assurance** is embedded in the **modular architecture**, with standardized extension points enforcing compliance through custom service integration and analytics. The framework’s reliance on **BIM’s static limitations** suggests potential fidelity gaps, necessitating supplementary validation for dynamic operational scenarios (e.g., TwinLight/BOREALIS projects)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** supporting this DT system is designed for **heterogeneous interoperability** and **scalability**, with the following technical specifics:
  - **Communication Protocols**: Aligned with **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, the system supports protocols enabling cross-platform integration (e.g., industry-standard protocols for IoT, BIM, or building automation), though exact protocols are not enumerated.
  - **Network Topology and Architecture**: The framework employs a **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points**, suggesting a **hybrid or service-oriented topology** where components (e.g., BIM tools, IoT sensors, analytics services) communicate via the central DT hub. The **bidirectional data exchange** implies a **star or mesh topology** for real-time synchronization.
  - **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**: Critical for **real-time monitoring and control**, but exact thresholds (e.g., <100ms latency, 10Mbps bandwidth) are not specified. The emphasis on **runtime adaptability** suggests dynamic resource allocation.
  - **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**: Implicit in the **scalability and maintainability** requirements (NF_X), though no specific redundancy strategies (e.g., failover nodes, data replication) are described.
  - **Security Protocols**: Not explicitly detailed, but the framework’s **accessibility for varying technical expertise** and **interoperability** imply adherence to industry security standards (e.g., encryption, access control) for network communication.
  "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for this DT system is designed to support **dynamic operational needs** with the following technical attributes:
  - **Hosting Platforms**: Supports **cloud, edge, and on-premise** deployment models, though no specific platforms (e.g., AWS IoT, Kubernetes, local servers) are mentioned. The **modular architecture** and **runtime configuration management** enable flexible hosting choices.
  - **Computational and Storage Requirements**: Scalable to handle **individual components to entire infrastructure systems**, with low-overhead updates ensuring maintainability. Exact resource specifications (e.g., CPU cores, storage tiers) are not provided.
  - **Scalability Mechanisms**: Enabled by the **modular design** and **standardized extension points**, allowing incremental scaling of services and analytics without full redeployment. The framework’s **adaptability** suggests auto-scaling or elastic resource allocation.
  - **Deployment Models Supported**: Implicitly includes **hybrid models** (e.g., cloud-edge collaboration) and **on-premise** for sensitive or low-latency applications, though no specific deployment frameworks (e.g., Docker, Terraform) are referenced.
  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance**: Addressed through **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, with mechanisms for continuous synchronization and runtime adjustments. No explicit SLAs (e.g., 99.9% uptime) or redundancy strategies (e.g., multi-region hosting) are detailed.
  "
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates horizontally with **external systems** through **interoperability standards** and **data exchange protocols**, ensuring seamless connectivity across heterogeneous environments. Key integration aspects include:
  - **BIM Tools**: Building Control Engineers use BIM platforms (e.g., Revit, ArchiCAD) to model devices and deploy Digital Twins, enabling **static-to-dynamic data translation** via enriched BIM models.
  - **Enterprise Systems**: The framework supports integration with **legacy and modern enterprise systems** (e.g., CMMS, ERP) through **standardized extension points**, allowing for **bidirectional data flows** (e.g., asset metadata, operational logs).
  - **Interoperability Standards**: The system adheres to **non-functional requirements (NF_X)** addressing cross-system compatibility, including **scalability** (from components to infrastructure) and **accessibility** for users with varying technical expertise.
  - **Dependencies**: External systems may provide **sensor data**, **control signals**, or **analytical services**, with the DT Framework managing dependencies via **runtime configuration** and **modular service integration**. Integration patterns include **event-driven updates** (e.g., real-time alerts) and **batch synchronization** (e.g., historical data for analytics). The system avoids vendor lock-in by prioritizing **open standards** and **low-overhead updates** for maintainability."
]
